Hey, what's going on everyone? Okay, so special notes, special considerations for this. Change is hard, emotionally. So I just want you to be prepared for that. Like change is difficult. It is taxing on you. Anything that's new can be difficult. Next one is that not everyone will say yes. So if you're converting your membership over, not everyone will say yes. And so you might think in your head, okay, well if I keep 25% of people, I'll be happy. But if you have 200 members, that means 50 people are gonna say no. Mathematically, 100% makes sense and you absolutely should make that change. But I want you to know that when you have 50 people say no, it hurts. And so you have to be prepared for that, right? Like it's the hard truth, okay? It's a numbers game, right? And so you gotta be willing to sacrifice what is good right now for what can be great. And that's not just for you, but for what your customers desire too. Like group workouts are great, but they're commodity and they're gonna continue to go down in price, whereas one-on-one and the accountability is gonna be huge. So just think about this way, if you're making the full EFT flip, that you need to donate your old model to jumpstart your new model. That doesn't have nearly the overhead or risk, okay? When you are selling this, 101, you have to come from a space of absolute conviction, 100%, not confidence, certainty, all right? So if you're not sure, like if you feel like you're just gonna, like I'm telling you, if you think that coaching people 101 is just gonna be like five minutes a week, like you're wrong. That's not gonna work that way. But you're still getting a massive margin on your time or your coach's time by doing it this way. And so like coaching people online takes work. I have an online business, it takes work. So just don't get sold into some dream. Like it's just a better profit model. It does not mean that work will not be there, all right? So you need to understand, like if you need to go through the fulfillment piece to understand how valuable what you're gonna be providing is, then go through that so that you can have the conviction that you're actually gonna help people in the real world in the long term, right? Like right now, if your parents can't go to your gym, like that's a problem, right? There's so many people who need help, who don't fit within our vehicle of fitness, right? But we have the opportunity, we have the skill set and the knowledge to help them and we just choose not to. And so this way we can help them in a way that is significant and sustainable over not like six months, but six years, right? 101 is what's going to help people make a sustainable change period. And you have to believe that so that when you're looking someone in the eye, you're like, this is going to help you. This is a better model. This is like, this is going to give you exactly what you need. And if you make it feel like you're just doing something for the money, you're going to fail, all right? I'm telling you, you're going to fail unless you do this from the right head space, all right? Well, I'm not saying you're going to fail. It's a very good model, like you'll be fine. But like, you will be far more successful and this will be far less stressful for you if you come from a place of absolute certainty knowing that your goal is to help this person and this is the best way to do it, okay? So the easiest way to think about this and when you're kind and conversation with a person is thinking about things in 10-year windows, not six weeks, okay? The person's been with you for six months, great. But like, how long have they been struggling for things? Do they think they're going to wake up at 5 a.m. for the rest of their life? Probably not, right? Or do they think they're going to be able to follow the templated meal plan for the rest of their life? Probably not, right? And so if you can teach them the skills, you can teach them to fish rather than not, right? Then you'll actually be able to actually help them for life, okay? So this is just a little section that we got back from the first gyms we went through in tradition to hybrid. I said, what would you have told yourself if you, like, knowing what you know now, what you've told yourself right as you were starting? First one across the board was like, relax, don't stress so much. It's going to be okay, all right? It's not going to be bad. It's going to be fine, all right? Once you start talking to people, you realize like, you like these people and they like you and like, you're doing fine, okay? 5% of people are crazy. Don't let it eff with you, all right? So like, just accept that. You have 5% of people who are just crazy and you're called an idiot and think that you don't know what you're doing and that they know more about business and blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, right? Know your reason why and story for making the change and that's kind of comes from that conviction piece I was just talking about. Like, you have to come from a place of true conviction of why you're making this change, right? And the change should be, hopefully, that this is a superior way for you to help people and fulfill the promises that you made to them and that you no longer want to provide a mediocre service and that you want to provide personalized attention on demand for people so that you can help them in a sustainable way the way they want to be helped. All right, hopefully you've had an epiphany about why this will help them in the long run and you should explain your thought process of how this is going to help and why what you're currently doing is inferior to what this is, all right? That's why I don't believe you can go half in. Like, you have to burn your boats. If you're going to make the swap, you have to swap. You have to no longer do groups so that you can explain why this is so much better, all right? Why this is superior, why you no longer do that. If you're doing bolts on, that's fine. You don't need to poo poo the large group but you're just going to be more selling the fact that that model doesn't allow you to have the one-on-one accountability and that is really what people need. 10% of it's from fitness. These are the other pieces of it, at least. You know what I mean? That should explain that to you. And you'll also see, this is feedback from everyone, it's like once you find your groove, you'll be amazed how easy it is. So like the first 10, like relax. Like you'll find your way and then once you find your groove, just knock it out as fast as you can and so that's actually the last piece which I thought there was a slide here but I'll reinforce it now that we're talking. You want to go fast and you want to go hard, all right? Think about this as a lightning surge. So if you're making the swap, you got to go fast and hard. That means long days, back-to-back meetings and get it done as fast as you can possibly. You don't want this to linger over three weeks, all right? You want to knock this out in seven days, five days if you can, and that means you're meeting with a ton of people. Yes, but it means that you can control the conversation, you can control the flow, you control the vibe in a much more significant way. That means that by two days in, you've already talked to half your gym members. You know what I mean? If you can, if you have a wife or a partner or a trainer that you trust a lot, you can even divide and conquer and talk to two people every 30 minutes and just knock these out, all right? So that is kind of the overview and just advice from the gyms who've gone through it is like relax, it's not gonna be bad. Like people are crazy, know your story. Like you have to have already talked through it, explain your real epiphanies, be convicted. And once you find a groove, you'll be amazed how easy it is. And Speed is King, once you go, go all in, burn your boats and do it. On the bolt-on side, I mean, Speed is King is good in general, but there's no real story that you need to get in front of there. You know what I mean? It's just you're gonna be adding more services to the gym and you're gonna try and meet with everybody individually to help them out. And I want to give you this piece that if someone doesn't come in for that meeting, those are the people who need the most help, all right? So I'll leave you with that.
